papers.title: Exploring nucleon-nucleon collisions and clusterization within the antisymmetrized molecular dynamics transport model by means of a Bayesian analysis on a dataset from the FAZIA setup
papers.authors: S. Piantelli, A. Camaiani, G. Poggi, A. Ono, L. Baldesi, S. Barlini, B. Borderie, R. Bougault, G. Casini, C. Ciampi, Q. Fable, C. Frosin, J. A. Dueñas, D. Gruyer, B. Hong, A. Kordyasz, N. Le Neindre, T. Marchi, S. H. Nam, J. Park, M. Pârlog, G. Pasquali, S. Valdré, G. Verde, and E. Vient (FAZIA Collaboration)
papers.summary: Author(s): S. Piantelli, A. Camaiani, G. Poggi, A. Ono, L. Baldesi, S. Barlini, B. Borderie, R. Bougault, G. Casini, C. Ciampi, Q. Fable, C. Frosin, J. A. Dueñas, D. Gruyer, B. Hong, A. Kordyasz, N. Le Neindre, T. Marchi, S. H. Nam, J. Park, M. Pârlog, G. Pasquali, S. Valdré, G. Verde, and E. Vient (FAZIA Collaboration)The in-medium nucleon-nucleon cross section and the cluster production are key ingredients in transport models for heavy-ion collisions. They have been constrained by the comparison of predictions of the AMD transport model to experimental data of $^{20}\mathrm{Ne}+^{12}\mathrm{C}$ at 50 MeV/nucleon…[Phys. Rev. C 113, 064622] Published Fri Jun 26, 2026

papers.title: Systematic study of flow of protons and light clusters in intermediate-energy heavy-ion collisions with momentum-dependent potentials
papers.authors: Viktar Kireyeu, Vadim Voronyuk, Michael Winn, Susanne Gläßel, Jörg Aichelin, Christoph Blume, Elena Bratkovskaya, Gabriele Coci, and Jiaxing Zhao
papers.summary: Author(s): Viktar Kireyeu, Vadim Voronyuk, Michael Winn, Susanne Gläßel, Jörg Aichelin, Christoph Blume, Elena Bratkovskaya, Gabriele Coci, and Jiaxing ZhaoWe study the influence of the nuclear equation of state (EOS) on collective observables—the directed (${v}_{1}$) and elliptic flow (${v}_{2}$) of nucleons and light clusters—in heavy-ion collisions at gigaelectronvolt energies energies using the parton-hadron-quantum-molecular dynamics (PHQMD) appro…[Phys. Rev. C 113, 064909] Published Fri Jun 26, 2026
